nameMeanings.name: Bryan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Bryant  .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Briain, see O'Brien .

variants.name: Bryant
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Celtic personal name Brian (from brigo- ‘high’ + the suffix -ant-), with excrescent -t. Breton bearers of this name were among the Normans who invaded England in 1066. They went on to settle in Ireland in the 12th century, where the name mingled with the native Irish form Briain (see O'Brien ). The latter had also been borrowed, as Brján, by the Vikings, who introduced it independently into northwestern England before the Norman Conquest.
Breton: very rare variant of   Briant   (see   Briand  ) and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: The American poet William Cullen Bryant (1794–1878) came of a New England family, being descended from Stephen Bryant, who had settled in Plymouth Colony in 1632.

variants.name: Brine
meanings: English (southern): from an early Middle English personal name Brine (Old English Bryni), a pet form of names in Brūn from brūn ‘brown’.
Welsh: possibly a variant of   Bryan   or   O'Brien   as a result of Irish immigration into southwest Wales, or a habitational name from the Pembrokes lordship of Dyffryn Breuan.
In some cases possibly also an Americanizedd form of German Brein, a variant of Breun .

nameMeanings.name: Guy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of a compound name starting with witu ‘wood’ or wīt ‘wide’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England. In Old French initial w- regularly became gu-. The usual Norman forms of the name were Gy or Guido. In medieval Latin the same name is found as Wido. It was a popular name among the Normans, enhanced no doubt by the romance of Guy of Warwick, recounting the exploits of a folk hero of the Crusades.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Guy Bryan was born about 1289, in Castle, Montgomeryshire, Wales as the son of Sir Guy Bryan III and Sibil de Sully. He married Gwenllian verch Gruffudd of Wales about 1308. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died after 27 January 1344, in Torbryan, Devon, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Guy Bryan(은)는 대략 1289년 웨일스 몽고머이실르 카스틀에서 Sir Guy Bryan III(와)과 Sibil de Sully의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1308년 Gwenllian verch Gruffudd of Wales(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1344년 1월 27일 이후 영국 데폰 토르브라이아에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Guy Bryan родился около 1289 г. в местности кастл, монтгомиришир, Уэльс. Его родителей звали Sir Guy Bryan III и Sibil de Sully. Он женился на женщине по имени Gwenllian verch Gruffudd of Wales около 1308 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер после 27 январь 1344 г. в местности торбрян, девон, Англия.
